svg.text: • find(x) — return the root of x's set
• union(x, y) — merge the sets containing x and y
• connected(x, y) — true iff find(x) == find(y)
Two optimisations make it nearly O(1):
• Path compression — flatten on every find
• Union by rank / size — attach smaller tree under larger root
Combined: O(α(n)) per operation. α = inverse Ackermann ≤ 4 in practice.
• Online connectivity — edges arrive one by one, query is "same component?"
• Kruskal's minimum spanning tree — sort edges, union those that join components
• Detect cycle in undirected graph — union returns false if already same set
• Equivalence relations / merging buckets — string equivalence, account merging
• Number-of-components in dynamic graphs — track count, decrement on each union
If the question is "are these in the same set?" — Union-Find.
